summary: This legacy tweak ID previously pointed at a per-user Explorer TaskbarDa write for the Widgets taskbar button. It was retired on 2026-03-13 because the app already ships a validated official machine-policy Widgets control under visibility.disable-widgets. Keep this record only as an audit trail from the old duplicate taskbar-only tweak name to the supported Widgets policy record.

setting.name: Taskbar Widgets Entry Point (Deprecated Duplicate Reference)
setting.casual_explanation: This legacy tweak has been retired. Use visibility.disable-widgets for the supported Widgets policy.
setting.why_people_change_it: This record is no longer used for product or research decisions because the app now keeps a single official Widgets control instead of a duplicate taskbar-only variant.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Keeping a deprecated duplicate reference preserves the old tweak identity for audit purposes while preventing future work from treating the undocumented TaskbarDa parent-key write as the supported Widgets control.
setting.professional_notes: The retired implementation wrote HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced -> TaskbarDa = 0 as a REG_DWORD value. Official Microsoft sources instead document the Widgets feature policy at HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Dsh\AllowNewsAndInterests and separately document a Windows 11 settings-reference TaskbarDa subkey with REG_SZ semantics. Because the app already ships visibility.disable-widgets for the official policy surface, this duplicate Explorer tweak was removed from VisibilityTweakProvider.cs on 2026-03-13 instead of being migrated to a second copy of the same policy.

targets.path: Widgets research duplicate retirement audit trail

allowed_values.label: Deprecated duplicate tweak
allowed_values.meaning: This record exists only as an audit trail. Use visibility.disable-widgets for the supported Widgets policy surface.

targets.notes: Synthetic audit-only target added to preserve the old tweak ID without treating the retired TaskbarDa write as a live supported control.

app_current_implementation.notes: The duplicate explorer.disable-taskbar-widgets provider entry was removed on 2026-03-13. The supported Widgets implementation remains visibility.disable-widgets, which writes the official machine policy AllowNewsAndInterests = 0.

windows_defaults.label: Deprecated audit trail

states.rationale: This record remains only to preserve the retired tweak ID and point future research to visibility.disable-widgets.

windows_defaults.notes: No live OS behavior is modeled here anymore; the Widgets feature is covered by visibility.disable-widgets.

recommended_profiles.label: Use visibility.disable-widgets
intended_for: Researchers following the legacy taskbar-only tweak ID
avoid_for: Anyone looking for a current standalone Explorer Widgets toggle

states.rationale: The supported Widgets path lives in visibility.disable-widgets and this record exists only to preserve the old ID.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You need an audit trail from the retired duplicate Explorer tweak to the supported Widgets policy record.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You are trying to make current apply or publication decisions from this deprecated record.
tradeoffs: Preserves the old taskbar-only tweak identity for audit and migration history.
Avoids keeping two live tweaks that target the same Widgets feature outcome.

evidence.title: Widgets duplicate retirement audit trail
evidence.summary: explorer.disable-taskbar-widgets was retired on 2026-03-13 because the app already ships visibility.disable-widgets for the official Widgets policy surface. The old TaskbarDa parent-key write was removed from VisibilityTweakProvider.cs and this record now remains only as an audit trail.

decision.why: Deprecated after the duplicate Explorer tweak was removed. The app already has a validated official Widgets control under visibility.disable-widgets, so keeping a second taskbar-only tweak would duplicate behavior and preserve an undocumented legacy surface.

The old TaskbarDa parent-key write was removed from VisibilityTweakProvider.cs and this record now remains only as an audit trail.
